label: About
title: About Ceramic Plinth Rupture
short_blurb: A daily generated front page where the image is the interface.
body: Daily Frontpage is a daily generated interactive front page. Each edition starts from recent saved signals and source research, becomes a new image-led scene, and stays explorable in the archive. The image is the interface: visible marks, objects, and surfaces in the scene open the real sources that shaped the day.

This iteration began with 81 recent saved-signal files and 69 captured links, then narrowed the page to 9 source windows around @digitlartifacts: gorgeous ceramics from artist hedy yang and Anchor image material 2, plus 7 more. A source image from pbs.twimg.com helped set the palette, contrast, edge behavior, and gesture. The scene became Ceramic Plinth Rupture: Bright ceramic product-photo rupture, oblique product table: one impossible-scale rounded vessel violently cropped through the frame, its cloudy teal belly carrying apertures and glaze chips; two smaller pastel vessels process behind it across a broken white plinth line. Preserve airy catalog-white gutters, soft rim... After the image was made, a plate-reading pass mapped 9 visible regions and the mask audit tightened them against large blue storm-belly aperture and weathered glaze, rear peach vessel rim shadow and side pinhole, and front round vessel dark mouth and blue-orange glaze field.

typography.rationale: Bricolage Grotesque and Space Grotesk fit record, arcade, and listening-room editions while Fraunces keeps source captions tactile.
